---
title: "Welcome to the Turner Lab" 
image: images/lab_logo_text
about: 
  template: jolla
---

![Lab photo, Spring 2025](images/labphoto2025.jpg){style="float: right;" fig-alt="Turner lab photo, Spring 2025" fig-align="right" width="250"}


**The Turner lab is a research lab at the Ohio State University in the Department of Psychiatry. The main mission of the lab is ____. Our goal is to ___.** 


## Research Areas 
------------------------------------------------------------------------

**The brain is an ongoing puzzle!** Our research primarily uses neuroimaging techniques to study the brain mechanisms of psychiatric disorders, particularly schizophrenia and psychosis generally, and neuropsychiatric symptoms in neurological disorders. Learn more [here](research.qmd).



## Current Projects
------------------------------------------------------------------------

* Cerebellar contributions to psychosis
* Cerebellum and aging
* Analyses to Reveal Trajectories and Early Markers of Imminent Shifts in Suicidal States [ARTEMIS study website](artemis-study.org)
* Enhancing Neuroimaging Genetics through Meta/Mega Analysis (ENIGMA) Schizophrenia (for more information see the ENIGMA working groups website: [ENIGMA Working Groups](https://enigma.ini.usc.edu/ongoing/)



## Collaborators
------------------------------------------------------------------------

* List collaborators

University link: [Department of Psychiatry and Behavioral Health Faculty Page](https://medicine.osu.edu/find-faculty/clinical/psychiatry-and-behavioral-health/jessica-turner-phd)
